message:
feat(focus): add host_focused state tracking for correct blur/restore behavior when terminal loses focus

FocusManager now tracks whether the host terminal window has focus via a
new host_focused field. This prevents several incorrect behaviors when
the terminal is backgrounded:

- push_trap no longer force-focuses the first group member when the host
  is unfocused (would cause a jarring visual flash when the terminal
  regains focus)
- pop_trap blurs instead of restoring return_focus when unfocused
- repair_focus_after_excluding_ids, restore_focus_after_invalid_current,
  and repair_focus_after_group_change all short-circuit to blur when the
  host is unfocused — previously they would attempt focus restoration
  that the user couldn't see, leaving stale visual indicators

Implementation:
- host_focused field (default: true) with getter/setter
- apply_host_focus now persists the state alongside its existing blur
  behavior
- push_trap refactored: extract push_trap_with_return_focus as a
  pub(crate) building block that separates trap registration from focus
  targeting — ModalFocusCoordinator uses this to control return_focus
  precisely during out-of-order modal pops
- Minor indentation fix in is_current_focusable_in_group
- Tab navigation wrap: extract first/last from order into named locals

Modal integration (focus_integration.rs, stack.rs):
- ModalFocusCoordinator updated to use push_trap_with_return_focus
  for explicit return-focus control during modal push/pop sequences
- ModalStack gains host-focus-aware event dispatch

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
